Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 224. The King’s Work is a Burden on Me (ABL 0888)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

a-na LUGAL EN-[a]

(1) To the king, my lord: your servant Samnu[ha-beli-uṣ]ur.

o 22

ARAD-ka msa-am-nu-[ḫu—U]—⸢PAB⸣

o 33

DI-mu a-na LUGAL EN-a

(3) Good health to the king, my lord!

o 44

[dul]-⸢lu⸣ LUGAL ina UGU-ḫi-a KALAG-an

(4) The king's work has become a burden to me. [I released] my [me]n in early winter, but those who were released entered strongholds in order to evade the king's work. [O king my lo]rd, let a royal bodyguard be commissioned to bring them out, and let them do the king's [work].

o 55

[LÚ.ERIM]-MEŠ-a ku-ṣi* ḫar-pi*

o 66

<$x x x x$>

o 77

⸢ša*⸣ i-pa-ṭar-u-ni

o 88

[TA] ⸢IGI*⸣ dul-li MAN

o 99

[ina] ⸢ŠÀ* É⸣.KALAG-MEŠ-ma e-tar-bu

o 1010

[LUGAL be]-lí LÚv.qur-bu-ti

o 1111

[lip]-⸢qi⸣-du lu-še-ṣa-šú-nu

o 1212

[dul-lu] ša LUGAL

o 1313

[le]-pu-šu

o 1414

[m]dsa-am-nu-ḫu—bé-su-nu

(14) Samnuhu-bessunu, a citizen of Šadikanni

o 1515

⸢URU*⸣.še*-di-kan*-a.a

Bottom
b.e.b.e. uninscribed

Reverse
r 1r 1

[x x x x x]+⸢x⸣ me*

(r 1) [......] holds [...; ...] have passed into the possession of the people of Kannu'.

r 22

[x x x] ú-kal

r 33

[x x]+⸢x⸣ ŠÀ* ina KI.⸢TA⸣

r 44

[LÚv].⸢kan*⸣-nu-uʾ-a.a

r 55

e-tar-bu

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P334610/.
